steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dialog of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, selecting the agent type for Microsoft 365 Copilot.
Click the "No Action" option in the dropdown menu within the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the workspace folder selection dropdown within Visual Studio Code, selecting the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects" as the project root.
Type  ${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}}  into the "Input an application name" text field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension popup within Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to confirm the application name ${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}} in the input dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion there's readme file opened and the content in the preview README tab can be displayed normally.
Click the close (“X”) button at the top-right corner of the “Preview README.md” tab in Visual Studio Code, closing the preview of the README file.
Click the "Create a New Agent/App" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar of Visual Studio Code, initiating a new agent or app creation workflow.

Click the "Custom Engine Agent" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code, selecting the agent type for building an intelligent agent with orchestration and your own LLM.
Click the "Basic Custom Engine Agent" dropdown option in the "App Features Using Microsoft 365 Agents SDK" extension interface within Visual Studio Code, targeting the agent selection menu.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default Folder" dropdown entry in the "Workspace Folder" selection dialog within Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the highlighted crosshair at (283, 75).
Type  ${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}}  into the "Input an application name" text field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension popup within Visual Studio Code.

Type the character 'a' into the "Application Name" input field at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code, which currently contains the text "vscuse_app_NgmR4" and displays a path conflict warning.
Press Enter to confirm the application name in the input dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion there's readme file opened and the content in the preview README tab can be displayed normally.
Click the "Close" (X) button at the top-right corner of the Visual Studio Code window, located on the title bar, to exit the application.
Click the "Create a New Agent/App" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome panel within Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (197, 302).

Click the "Copilot connector" option in the "New Project" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, targeting the UI element to embed organization data for Microsoft 365 Copilot integration.
Type text 'testconnectorname' into the "Add a connector name" input field within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Press the Enter key to confirm the connector name input in the "Add a connector name" dialog within Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Type text 'testconnectionid' into the "Add a connection ID" input field at the top of the Microsoft Visual Studio Code window, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Press the Enter key to confirm the connection ID input in the "Add a connection ID" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the folder selection dropdown within the "Workspace Folder" dialog of Visual Studio Code, choosing the path /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects.
@assertion there's readme file opened and the content in the preview README tab can be displayed normally.
Click the close (×) button on the "Preview README.md" tab in the Visual Studio Code interface to close the Markdown preview.
Click the "Create a New Agent/App" blue button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" pop-up menu within the Visual Studio Code extension for Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit. The selection is indicated by a red crosshair.

Click the "General Teams Agent" dropdown option in the modal titled "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface. The selected option highlights the description: "Agent that chats with users in Teams built with Teams AI Library and connects to LLMs."
Click the "Tab" option in the dropdown menu of the "Teams Capability" selection dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default Folder" option in the folder selection dropdown of the "Workspace Folder" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Type  ${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}}  into the "Input an application name" text field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension popup within Visual Studio Code.
Type 'b' into the "Application Name" input field at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code, where the current value is "vscuse_app_6ntwp".
Press Enter to confirm the application name in the input dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion there's readme file opened and the content in the preview README tab can be displayed normally.
Click the "Close" button (window control with an 'X' icon) at the top-right corner of the Visual Studio Code window to exit the application.
Click the "Create a New Agent" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome panel within Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (233, 300).
Click the "Office Add-in" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Task pane" entry in the dropdown menu under "Select a capability" within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code. The click is visually indicated by a red crosshair on the highlighted dropdown option.
Click the "Default folder" dropdown in the Workspace Folder selection dialog of Visual Studio Code, targeting the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects" to choose the project location.
Type  ${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}}  into the "Input an application name" text field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension popup within Visual Studio Code.
Type the letter 'c' into the "Application Name" input field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface within Visual Studio Code, where a path conflict warning is displayed.
Press Enter to confirm the application name in the input dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion there's readme file opened and the content in the preview README tab can be displayed normally.
Click the red close button in the top-right corner of the Visual Studio Code window to close the application.
